name.value: Blue Canyon Country Club — Lakes Course

green_fee_weekday_thb.note: Low season (Apr–Oct) green fee only, excl. compulsory caddie ฿400 and cart ฿700. High season rate TBC — tracked in data-gaps.

green_fee_weekend_thb.note: High season (Nov–Mar) rate not confirmed. Tracked in data-gaps.md.

prose.overview: Blue Canyon Country Club's Lakes Course is the younger sibling to the celebrated Canyon Course, sharing the same Thalang property and clubhouse facilities but offering a distinctly different golf experience. Designed by Yoshikazu Kato and opened in February 1999, the Lakes Course takes its name from the series of large, scenic lakes that define the layout — former tin mine excavations transformed into sweeping water hazards. At 7,129 yards from the championship tees, the Lakes Course is marginally shorter than the Canyon Course, with a more open, resort-style character. It tends to attract players who want a high-quality round in a spectacular setting without the same degree of technical difficulty as the Canyon. The significantly lower green fee makes it an excellent-value option for a second day of golf or for visitors whose handicap would find the Canyon Course punishing.
prose.layout_and_experience: The par-72 layout is defined by water: multiple holes require accurate drives and approach shots over or alongside large lakes, and the risk/reward element is consistently present without becoming oppressive. The fairways are more generous than the Canyon Course and the terrain flatter, making it more walker-friendly — though carts are still compulsory. Greens are large and true, rewarding consistent ball-striking over the punch-and-run strategy that works well on the Canyon. For higher handicappers, the Lakes Course is the more comfortable of the two Blue Canyon tracks — enough water to keep things interesting, but manageable for visitors who score in the 90s. For low handicappers, the course's width and pace of play make it a good option for working on course management or playing a casual final-day round. The shared facilities with the Canyon Course — driving range, practice putting green, restaurant, spa, and accommodation — are among the best available at any Phuket golf venue.
prose.tips: The Lakes Course is an ideal choice if you're planning two rounds at Blue Canyon: start with Lakes in the morning and follow up with the Canyon Course in the afternoon, or on a second day. Tee times are generally easier to secure here than for the Canyon, even during high season. For golfers prone to losing balls on water holes, bring extra sleeves — several long par-4s require carries over lake edges from the tee. Ask your caddie about the wind: the open layout means breeze affects approach shots more than on the more sheltered Canyon Course. Green fees are substantially lower than the Canyon Course, making this an exceptional-value round by Phuket standards.
prose.location_and_access: The Lakes Course sits within the Blue Canyon Country Club property at 165 Moo 1, Thepkasattri Road, Thalang district, approximately 10 km from Phuket International Airport — around 15–20 minutes by car. All visitors from Bangkok fly (approximately 1 hour 20 minutes). From Patong Beach, the drive is approximately 35–40 minutes. Grab or hotel transfers are the recommended transport; both courses share the same clubhouse and car park.
prose.rental_cta_context: Playing Blue Canyon's Lakes Course during your Phuket trip? If your route includes Bangkok, rent premium clubs delivered to your hotel — skip the baggage fees and arrive ready to play.
